text.title: Impl Plan Examples Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Impl Plan Examples. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Maintain exactly four worked impl-plan examples: greenfield, refactor, migration, infra.
rule.rationale: Four archetypes cover ~85% of impl-plan cases.

rule.statement: Every example has full TASK list, token estimates, and dependency graph; no placeholders.
rule.rationale: Placeholders signal 'optional'; full content sets the bar.

rule.statement: Example version matches writing-implementation-plans; updates sync in the same PR.
rule.rationale: Drift between layout and examples confuses authors.

rule.statement: Author records the archetype picked in artefact front-matter; 'none' is valid but explicit.
Every TASK is either adapted or marked 'kept from example'; verbatim copies flagged.
rule.rationale: Verbatim copies signal lack of engagement.

rule.statement: Examples reviewed quarterly to align with current stack.
If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-impl-plan-examples.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
